question_title: Will the interest in “consigliere” change between 2026-02-17 and 2026-02-27 according to Google Trends?
summary: Robert Duvall (who played consigliere Tom Hagen in The Godfather) died on Feb 16, 2026, causing "consigliere" search interest to spike to 100. Feb 17 shows 91 - still riding the spike. By Feb 27 (11 days after the celebrity death spike), interest will have decayed back to baseline (~10-15). The resolution compares values in the Jan 28-Feb 27 window where the Feb 16 peak anchors the scale at 100. The expected drop of ~70-80 points from Feb 17 to Feb 27 massively exceeds the ±3 threshold. The Oscars (where a tribute could briefly re-spike interest) aren't until March 15. This is a textbook post-celebrity-death decay pattern, making "Decreases" overwhelmingly likely.

factors.description: Robert Duvall died Feb 16 causing the spike - Feb 17 is at 91, a celebrity death news spike that will decay to baseline within days
Baseline for 'consigliere' is ~10-15; by Feb 27 (day 11 post-death), interest will have returned to this level, creating a ~70-80 point drop far exceeding the ±3 threshold
Oscars ceremony is March 15 (after resolution), removing the main potential catalyst for a second spike within the resolution window
Possible funeral/memorial service could briefly boost interest around Feb 27, but such secondary events produce much smaller spikes than the death announcement
Change_stats base rates (25% increase, 24% decrease, 51% no change) are baseline rates that don't account for the current massive spike - the spike context dominates

reasoning:
Now let me analyze the resolution dynamics carefully.**Forecast: Decreases (92%), Doesn't change (4%), Increases (4%)**

The key driver is Robert Duvall's death on Feb 16, 2026. Duvall played Tom Hagen, the iconic consigliere in The Godfather, and his death caused "consigliere" search interest to spike from a baseline of ~10-15 to 100. Feb 17 (the start date) still shows 91 — riding the spike.

By Feb 27 (11 days after the spike), interest will have decayed back to baseline. Celebrity death search spikes typically return to normal within 3-5 days. The expected drop of ~70-80 points massively exceeds the ±3 threshold for "Decreases."

The only plausible scenario for a non-decrease would be another major event driving "consigliere" searches around Feb 27. The Oscars (where a tribute could re-spike interest) aren't until March 15 — well after resolution. A funeral/memorial could generate brief interest but wouldn't approach Feb 17 levels.
